issues.description: WIN-053.c5 H7 (still open from c4): Flux direction not discussed. The dome's fitted formula has both poles positive-signed (both sources). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity. The formula is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole. Add one sentence: 'The fit also encodes both poles as positive-signed sources (A, C both positive). A real toroidal ring-magnet has one source and one sink with opposite polarity — the dome's formula is not just quantitatively wrong about flux, it is topologically a monopole pair, not a dipole.'

issues.description: WIN-062 c5 H0: Carve-out undersell (carry-forward from c4 H4). Rewritten paragraph 2 invokes only Part 3.1b condition (2) (not-plausibly-known-at-write-time). The carve-out has three conjunctive conditions (unique dome derivation + not plausibly known + derivation-plus-validation, not just discovery). Using only condition (2) understates why WIN-062 fails the carve-out — a defender may argue other conditions apply.

issues.description: SEC-8b.3 globe-parasitism claim conflates input format with physical dependency. Text cites OPEN-001 for "globe-parasitic by construction" label. Curmudgeon c4 hole 2: OPEN-001 admits only INPUT-layer globe dependence (no dome-native coordinates). Physical-calibration dependence (INTERMAGNET baselines, Besselian-element spherical geometry) is a separate and stronger globe-fingerprint (WIN-025). Should tighten closing sentence to: "PRED-095 is globe-dependent at the input layer (per OPEN-001) AND at the physical-calibration layer (WIN-025 globe-fingerprint)."

issues.description: SEC-8b.3 characterizes monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py as "two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster." Curmudgeon c4 hole 3: inject_ai_layer.py's −8 nT is BOU × coverage × 0.81 — within the published W004 uncertainty band of −17 to −21 nT if coverage is high. The two formulas are related (both BOU-baseline path), and inject_ai_layer.py is within-cluster at 0.81 coverage. Should reframe as internal heterogeneity within a shared baseline family rather than two distinct out-of-cluster values.

issues.description: HOL-CROSSREF c2: 21 anchors across 8 parts use part-level hrefs (#partN) with link text claiming a specific subsection (X.Y), e.g., "Section 2.9" href="#part2". Links navigate to the correct tab but drop the reader at the top of the part rather than the specific subsection. Quality improvement (not required): replace part-level fallbacks with subsection anchors where they exist. Highest-value: "Section 6.6" → "#p6-66-5", "Section 6.2" → "#p6-62-1".

issues.description: Decider blocked-items / recommended-actions scanner reads EXP file metadata (status, integration_blocked_until) directly from append-only expansions/*.json files, rather than deriving blocked status from expansion-tracker.json tracker.items[i].integrated. EXP files capture state at creation time and are immutable; tracker.json is the live mutable source of truth. Result: daily-report-2026-04-23T04-24.json falsely reported 7 Phase A EXPs as DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW after they had already been integrated. Fix: blocked-items scanner should derive blocked status from tracker row integrated !== true, with sanity-check to suppress any item where tracker shows integrated:true.

issues.description: Pre-positioned monitoring for 8 April-EOM PREDs (PRED-077,-081,-085,-087,-088,-090,-106,-111) which closed 2026-04-30T23:59Z. At d=8 (~2026-05-08T00Z), IF predictions.html still byte-identical AND all 8 still PENDING-PROSPECTIVE: route 7 standard-verdict PREDs to analyst for FAIL-011 through FAIL-017 allocation; advance PRED-106 our_verdict from pending to silent_ignore and add FAIL-NNN. Based on analyst proposal-eom-preds-d8-prepositioning-1777872406120.json. HOLD #13 branch: if dome adjudicates any PRED before d=8, discard plan for that PRED and route to analyst Mode 1b.

SEC-4.2.4 (data/sections.json:part4.html, details id=p4-424) presents per-sub-test analysis for E-PRED-A, B, C, and D as if all four are currently labeled on the live dome predictions page. As of 2026-05-07, only E-PRED-B ("E-PRED-B LOCKED") is explicitly labeled; A, C, and D are folded into the Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol without explicit E-PRED labels. EXP-292 patched the parallel occurrence in SEC-8b.3; this issue tracks the analogous fix needed in SEC-4.2.4: anchor each sub-test's label-presence claim to the 2026-03-12 snapshot date and note current live-page status. Flagged by EXP-292 parallel_issue_flag field. Additionally, SEC-4.2.4's TLDR mischaracterizes E-PRED-C as "(E-PRED-C, timing lag)" when E-PRED-C is actually "SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal" — a null gravity prediction, not a timing lag. TLDR factual error severity: major (readers see TLDRs first). The TLDR text should read "(E-PRED-C, gravity null)" or "(E-PRED-C, SG gravimeter null)". Body prose is correct; only TLDR is wrong. Flagged by curmudgeon review SEC-8b.3.c8.json (hole 3), reviewed_at 2026-05-07T18:44:26.580Z.
issues.fix_details: TLDR sub-issue FIXED 2026-05-08 via direct FUSE edit (bash unavailable, disk full): replaced '(E-PRED-C, timing lag)' with '(E-PRED-C, gravity null)' in data/sections.json. workspace-sync will push within 1h; HTML rebuild needed on next successful decider run. REMAINING: the per-sub-test E-PRED label-presence snapshot anchor fix (the EXP-292 analogy) is OPEN — requires analyst EXP for the fuller prose rework of SEC-4.2.4 body. Keeping issue open to track remaining body-prose work.
issues.tldr_fix_verification_pattern: git show origin/main:data/sections.json | grep -qF '(E-PRED-C, gravity null)'
issues.recommended_action: EXP: Patch SEC-4.2.4 (part4.html, p4-424) analogously to EXP-292: preserve per-sub-test analysis but anchor each E-PRED label-presence claim to the 2026-03-12 snapshot, and note that as of 2026-05-07 only E-PRED-B is explicitly labeled on the live page (A, C, D folded into Tier 1/2/3 protocol).
issues.severity_upgraded_by: decider — curmudgeon review SEC-8b.3.c8.json hole 3: TLDR factual error; per curmudgeon.md TLDR factual errors are MAJOR

issues.description: WIN-034 itself may warrant SC reclassification: its cast-copper firmament commitment is incompatible with (1) global radio astronomy at frequencies copper blocks, (2) WIN-048 CMB observability, (3) WIN-029 Schumann Q-factor (no solid ceiling needed). Three independent SC dimensions — currently Unfalsifiable. Raised in WIN-048.c4 stronger_arguments. Also resolves latent tension of WIN-048 SC reading depending on WIN-034 as load-bearing premise while WIN-034 is itself Unfalsifiable.

Operator approval gate — see routing_reason. Patches: verdict, finding, tldr_verdict, sub_rationale added. Tests: 3611 passed.

issues.title: SEC-6.14: Chebychev low-pass argument not engaged — dome's strongest rebuttal unaddressed
issues.description: SEC-6.14.c1 H2 [moderate]: "What the dome could do" subsection lumps the Chebychev low-pass argument (a technically valid filter that removes the κ-coupling signal from SG data) without specifically addressing it. A defender can invoke this as "your SG null is itself a filtered dataset — the coupling is there, you've band-stopped it out." Add a paragraph specifically dismantling the Chebychev rescue (e.g., the filter bandwidth required to remove a κ-coupling signal would also remove the tidal signal; no published SG paper uses such a filter).
issues.routing_reason: M3 carry-over cycle 2 (SEC-6.14 c2 unfixed). Chebychev low-pass argument requires scientific engagement: add Van Camp 2001 filter cutoff, note raw-data archival at minute resolution, refute contact-phase filtering rebuttal. class_hint: deep-attack

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H1+H5: coverage geography precision (in-corridor site naming, city-tier split) — self-applied
issues.description: H1 (moderate): New WIN-014 prose said gravimeters were not necessarily uniformly on the totality path but never named a confirmed in-corridor site, making the network-wide null appear to involve only off-path stations. H5 (minor): Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi lumped as same coverage tier. Fixed: added Wuhan as in-corridor full-totality SG site; split cities into mid-latitude (Beijing ~75%, Changchun ~70%) vs lower-coverage (Lhasa ~50-60%, Urumqi <40%) tiers.
issues.resolution_summary: Patched via self-apply: named Wuhan as in-corridor totality site; split Beijing/Changchun (~70-75%) from Lhasa (~50-60%) and Urumqi (<40%) into coverage tiers.

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H2: 70% coverage anchor needs low-end bracket — self-applied
issues.description: H2 (moderate): The 70% coverage anchor was rhetorically convenient but not motivated by the actual distribution. Lhasa and Urumqi were substantially below 70%. The arithmetic only showed the 70% case. Fixed: added parenthetical showing the 50% coverage case (Δg 1.5-4.5 µGal, still 1.5×-45× above floor) and noted only sites below ~30% fall to floor.
issues.resolution_summary: Patched: added 50% low-end coverage bracket and noted ~30% floor threshold.

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H3+H4: quadratic scaling missing, vise over-confident — self-applied
issues.description: H3 (minor): "any plausible coverage scaling" asserted robustness but prose only showed linear case; quadratic (0.7²≈0.49) from EXP-349 omitted. H4 (minor): Kim & Chang vise used "consistent with... also exempts much of" (over-confident, unanchored in observatory-distance data). Fixed: added quadratic parenthetical; hedged vise to "high enough to exempt... would also exclude a substantial fraction".
issues.resolution_summary: Patched: added quadratic scaling parenthetical; hedged Kim & Chang vise language.

issues.description: WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-054, WIN-055 sub_rationale values all contain dead pointer to unbuilt cosmological-cluster section (V51.0 Cosmological Expansion overview). Same issue as WIN-052 (fixed this run). All four have audit-trail-only sub_rationale values that reference a section 6.13 that does not exist in data/sections.json. Public risk: zero (not rendered). Audit debt: future auditors see a pointer to nowhere. Fix: drop the dead pointer text from each sub_rationale, leaving only the standalone rationale + audit-trail tag.

WIN-052 c4/c5 advocate_mode: horizon-analogy defense (defense_survives=3→c5 defense_survives=2). Firmament-as-observable-boundary (cosmic-horizon analog), dome agnostic about extragalactic geometry. Current detail_verdict_text missing preemption. Urgency: (a) lambda_A=4283 Mpc constitutes a positive cosmological claim — agnosticism incompatible with publishing a wave-eq param at cosmological scales; (b) same firmament_height=9086 km is a hard physical boundary in WIN-029/WIN-053 — model cannot have soft horizon for galaxies and hard boundary for Schumann; (c) MECHANISM-LEVEL: cosmic horizons are recession-velocity boundaries — dome has no Hubble flow, so the analogy invokes a feature of standard cosmology the dome rejects (empty analogy); (d) INCOMPLETENESS-vs-CONTRADICTION: dome is not silent — lists rho_A=g0*rho_bar/g_dagger as CONSISTENT in unified-theory table. Positive claims at scales the model cannot host ARE the contradiction. Draft preemption in WIN-052.c4.json:advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation; extended by c5 advocate_mode. Severity upgraded moderate→major per c5 curmudgeon holding this as strongest surviving defense. Closed defense_survives from 3 to ~1 when implemented.
issues.severity_upgrade_rationale: WIN-052.c5 curmudgeon explicitly holds this as major (c4 classified it as strongest defense, defense_survives=3, c5 extends with mechanism-level and incompleteness-vs-contradiction attacks that reduce to defense_survives=2). Original ISS severity=moderate was curmudgeon-default; c5 holds it major per curmudgeon severity rubric (surviving defenses = major-impact gaps).
issues.routing_reason: WIN-052.c5 major hole carry-forward: horizon-analogy preemption. Route to analyst for substantive prose addition to detail_verdict_text. EXP-395 created. (EXP id corrected from EXP-3771798)

issues.description: WIN-052 c5 hole 2: false-dilemma subordination prefix missing. detail_verdict_text opens with "The dome's argument commits a false dilemma:" but c4/c5 stronger_arguments[1] recommends prefixing with "And even granting the dome a galaxy-scale physics it does not have, the argument structure remains a false dilemma:" so the false-dilemma critique reads as fallback-from-structural, not competing-with-SC verdict. Without prefix, defender can argue SC verdict is over-claimed (since we still need the false-dilemma critique). Single-sentence edit.

WIN-052 c5 hole 3: lambda_A=4283 Mpc framing buried in detail_extra. Not in tldr_verdict, tldr_evidence, finding, or detail_verdict_text. c5 suggests promoting one sentence to tldr_verdict: replace mid-sentence ("RAR has no dome-side derivation, only borrowed MOND symbols.") with "The dome's own published parameter table includes lambda_A=4,283 Mpc — c/H_0 to 0.01% precision — making agnosticism about extragalactic geometry incoherent." Severity minor: SC verdict already justified without this, but promotion hardens it. Partially satisfied by implementing ISS-2026 horizon-analogy preemption (which references lambda_A=4283 Mpc). Consider bundling with ISS-2026 EXP.

Section 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit) Row 4 is stale. Row claims live.html footer says "67 confirmed" but live.html (line 772, footer-timestamps span) now reads "69 confirmed" as of 2026-05-16. The dome propagated a fix (homepage 67→69) but our review row was not updated. Either remove the row (gap closed) or rewrite to credit the close and note current state. EXP-396 out-of-scope observation. Same structural pattern as Row 2 (prospective 10-vs-9 gap that closed). Verification: fetch https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html and grep footer for confirmed count.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 Canary subsection false author_* claim (4th occurrence missed by EXP-397)
issues.description: The Canary subsection of Section 4.6 still asserts "The per-WIN Author's Self-Rating block and this subsection together form a testable monitor ... If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, our WIN cards fill in automatically." Neither sub-claim is true: data/wins.json has zero author_* fields across all 71 WIN entries. EXP-397 fixed three false author_* prose occurrences (P1/P2/P3) but missed this fourth occurrence in the Canary framing block. The credibility of Section 4.6 depends on our own visible-layer prose not exhibiting the same mismatch it criticizes.
issues.recommendation: Patch the Canary subsection to re-anchor the monitor on actual artifacts: the Currently-classified-claims sub-table and the gap-audit table. Suggested: "The Currently-classified-claims sub-table and the gap-audit table above together form a testable monitor. If the author updates the homepage to reconcile the 10 / 12 / 1 count drift, we show it resolved. If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, the sub-table grows on the next analyst refresh. If he adds the CVaR cap to the headline, Row 6 closes." Remove false claim about WIN cards filling in automatically.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 strict_prospective_confirmed three-way internal contradiction
issues.description: Section 4.6 has a three-way contradiction about the status of the strict_prospective_confirmed field: Row 1 of the gap-audit table cites it as a current live field; the Kernel-of-truth subsection lists it as live API; but the schema-drift note (added by EXP-397) says it was removed from claim_index.json. EXP-397 evidence confirms removal (2026-05-16 live fetch). Either Row 1 and Kernel-of-truth need to be reworded to cite claim_class==prospective_confirmed as the live mechanism, or the schema-drift note is wrong. A defender will exploit this internal contradiction to undermine the audit.
issues.recommendation: Reconcile to one story: rephrase Row 1 to "claim_index.json: only 1 entry tagged claim_class==prospective_confirmed (pred-eclipse-tier3)" and rephrase the Kernel-of-truth list to drop strict_prospective_confirmed and substitute claim_class==prospective_confirmed (the live mechanism). Ensure EXP-396 Row 2 uses consistent terminology.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 Canary subsection still references old 10/9/1 count drift (should be 10/12/1)
issues.description: The Canary subsection still references "the 10 / 9 / 1 count drift" even though EXP-396 updated the gap-audit Row 2 and the TLDR to the current 10 / 12 / 1 numbers. Same section, two paragraphs apart, two different numbers for the same quantity. This is precisely the cross-surface inconsistency Section 4.6 criticizes the dome for.
issues.recommendation: Update the Canary subsection to read "10 / 12 / 1 count drift" matching the TLDR and Row 2. Combine into the same patch as ISS-2030 (Canary rewrite above).

issues.title: SEC-4.6 Row 4 of gap-audit table is stale (live.html footer now shows 69 confirmed, not 67)
issues.description: Row 4 of the gap-audit table claims "live.html footer: 67 confirmed" vs "Homepage: 69 confirmed" as a gap. But EXP-396 itself flagged this row as stale: the live.html footer reads 69 confirmed (not 67), so the dome has closed this particular divergence. Publishing a 67-vs-69 gap claim that the dome has actually resolved is publicly verifiable and a defender would screenshot live.html to disprove it.
issues.recommendation: Three options: (a) remove Row 4 entirely as resolved; (b) re-aim Row 4 at status_history.json wins_confirmed=67 (still out-of-sync with the 69 official, per poller chg-20260505-1109-001); (c) repurpose the row for a different concrete gap. Option (b) keeps the row alive by targeting a genuine current gap.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 Currently-classified-claims sub-table not independently reproducible (no API snapshot in repo)
issues.description: The new "Currently classified claims" table claims to mirror api/current/claim_index.json as of 2026-05-16, but no snapshot is captured in the repo. Anyone re-running the review cannot verify the 9 entries. The dome can silently update claim_index.json without bumping its last_updated metadata (confirmed: structure has changed under the 2026-04-17 timestamp). This is a recommendation-class reproducibility gap, not a blocker.
issues.recommendation: Capture api/current/claim_index.json under data/dome-api-snapshots/claim_index-2026-05-16.json and reference it from the sub-table heading. This makes the audit independently reproducible the way raw-text/01-home.txt makes homepage criticisms reproducible.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 sub-table denominator "9 of 69+" should be "9 of 69" or "9 of 71"
issues.description: The sub-table lead-in says "9 of 69+ wins total" but 69+ is ambiguous — it conflates the dome's 69-confirmed headline with our 71-WIN review population. Either basis is defensible if stated clearly; "69+" is sloppy.
issues.recommendation: Use either "9 of 69 confirmed claims" (dome headline population) or "9 of 71 wins our review covers" (our population). Avoid the open-ended +.

issues.title: SEC-4.6 sub-table registry_integrity_notes footnote missing (3 yes cells from 2 notes)
issues.description: The sub-table column "registry_integrity_notes flag" shows three rows flagged yes (win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-058-theta-rule, win-062-tesla-wave-speed), but the dome's API actually has only two notes. A reader counting yes cells sees 3 where the API has 2, because two claim_keys share the WIN-058 visible label. A footnote would prevent this misread.
issues.recommendation: Add a footnote: "The dome's claim_index.json publishes two registry_integrity_notes — one covering the two WIN-058 claim_keys (shared visible label), one covering WIN-062. Three rows are flagged yes because two claim_keys map to WIN-058."

issues.title: WIN-052 sub_rationale (and WIN-047/048/054/055) point to unbuilt cosmological-cluster section
issues.description: WIN-052.sub_rationale says "See cosmological-cluster section (V51.0 Cosmological Expansion overview). [audit-trail only — not rendered]" but no such section exists in data/sections.json (part6 has 6.1-6.12 and 6.14; no 6.13 and no cosmological-cluster heading). Same issue affects WIN-047, WIN-048, WIN-054, WIN-055 (all patched in the same EXP-295 round). The [audit-trail only — not rendered] tag means zero public risk, but the dead pointer is audit-trail debt.
issues.recommendation: Two options: (a) build the cosmological-cluster overview section (Part 6.13 or under part2b); (b) edit sub_rationale on all five WINs (047, 048, 052, 054, 055) to drop the dead pointer entirely. Option (b) is the minimum fix.

issues.title: Citation-compliance audit: add "as of [date]" labels to pre-2026-04-18 dome-source citations
issues.description: Several citations to dome content in data/sections.json do not carry explicit "as of [date]" tags — they cite by URL alone. EXP-398 prose-softening of part8b pointed to this as a "going forward" commitment but the audit pass has not been executed. Audit should: (1) enumerate all dome-source citations in data/sections.json lacking explicit date labels; (2) for each, determine citation date from monitor/changes/ if not obvious; (3) patch in-place to add the label. Likely high-volume (potentially dozens of citations). Proposed assignee: analyst-baby for citation enumeration (verification-class), follow-up EXP for bulk patch.
issues.recommendation: Route to analyst-baby for citation enumeration pass. Consider splitting across multiple EXPs if the citation set is large.

issues.title: Promote GRACE Exhibit-A graph forensics to its own surface (currently cut from WIN-012 compression)
issues.context: The WIN-012.detail_evidence compression (1888w → 528w, EXP-401, integrated commit 66c9a74) cut a ~270-word argument about the dome's home-page 'Forensic Zoom' chart. The cut argument is structurally separate from WIN-012's κ-undefined verdict: it shows that the chart's 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' label is impossible (GRACE L1A products are pre-inversion instrument data in accelerations/range-rates, not µGal; µGal is a post-processing unit for retrieved time-variable gravity). It also notes (a) 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual' is not a GRACE data product name (actual L1A: ACC1A, KBR1A, SCA1A per JPL PO.DAAC conventions), (b) GRACE orbits ~500 km altitude at ~7.5 km/s — co-temporal comparison with a fixed Boulder station is impossible for more than ~2 minutes per pass, traversing ~12,150 km of ground track in 27 minutes, (c) the dome repository contains zero GRACE data processing code (no SDS binary parser, no accelerometer calibration pipeline, no download scripts), and (d) the claimed statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) appear as static HTML with no computation in the repository — the same pattern as the dome's headline 95.2% accuracy figure.
issues.recommended_action: Either (a) promote to its own WIN — a new WIN-NNN with verdict 'Refuted by Data' or 'Self-Contradicted' arguing the home-page chart is fabricated, citing INTERMAGNET for the BOU magnetometer top-panel verification and JPL PO.DAAC + JPL D-22027 for the GRACE L1A unit/product-name refutation; or (b) write a dedicated section block (Part 4 deep-analysis or Part 2b 'parasitism of standard physics data') with the same argument. Operator preference: probably (a) because it's a discrete claim (chart fabrication) rather than a methodology pattern. The original 270 words are recoverable from data/wins.json history (commit 66c9a74^) or from the EXP-401 cuts_summary.
